articles.title: Elite Kickboxers Think Pereira Is in Trouble
articles.description: What if the fight everyone has already decided turns out differently? In this video, Chael Sonnen explores a scenario almost nobody is talking about: Ciryl Gane defeating Alex Pereira. While the MMA world is already looking ahead to future matchups involving Pereira, Chael explains why dismissing Gane could be a mistake. Drawing on Gane's history against Francis Ngannou, his experience at heavyweight, and insights from elite kickboxers who have faced Pereira, Chael lays out a compelling case for why this fight may be much closer than fans think. Chael also dives into the psychological side of fighting, revisiting Gane's loss to Jon Jones and explaining why confidence, familiarity, and mindset can be just as important as physical skills. With Gane bringing in a former Pereira opponent to help prepare, there may be more strategy behind this matchup than meets the eye. The biggest takeaway isn't that Gane will win—it's that the possibility deserves far more attention than it's getting. Sometimes the fight everyone overlooks is the one that changes everything. Subscribe for more UFC analysis, fight breakdowns, and insider perspectives from the world of MMA. #UFC #AlexPereira #CirylGane #MMA #ChaelSonnen #TomAspinall #KickboxingBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Belal Muhammad Faces an Impossible Question
articles.description: Belal Muhammad achieved the goal every fighter dreams about. The problem is what comes next. In this video, Chael Sonnen breaks down the difficult position Belal Muhammad finds himself in after reaching the top of the sport. Chael explains why winning a world championship can create a unique challenge that few athletes are prepared for, drawing comparisons to legends like Georges St-Pierre, Khabib Nurmagomedov, Conor McGregor, Fedor Emelianenko, and Alexander Gustafsson. He also examines how Belal's relationship with Islam Makhachev could complicate any future path back to UFC gold. Chael dives into the mental side of competition, discussing the loss of purpose that can come after achieving a lifelong goal. He explores the reality of five-round main events, the sacrifices required to remain at the top, and why some athletes struggle to find the same motivation once they've already reached the summit. This isn't just a conversation about Belal Muhammad. It's a look at what happens when a fighter finally gets everything he worked for and must decide whether the climb is worth making again. For more UFC analysis, fight breakdowns, and behind-the-scenes stories from the fight game, be sure to subscribe and join the conversation. #UFC #BelalMuhammad #IslamMakhachev #ChaelSonnen #MMA #UFCRankings #FightNews Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Chael Reveals His PED Stack
articles.description: It's another edition of Ask Uncle Chael, and nothing is off limits. Chael responds to questions about Alex Pereira's move to heavyweight, whether social media should influence title shots, the changing landscape for young fighters, and the all-time fantasy matchups he'd most like to see. He also discusses Tom Aspinall and the White House card, Sean Strickland's invitation status, and why MMA opportunities are greater today than at any point in the sport's history. Plus, Chael shares stories from a very different era of MMA, reflects on the evolution of performance enhancement in combat sports, and answers whether he'd ever return to face Conor McGregor. Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Josh Hokit Is One Win Away
articles.description: Alex Pereira may have accidentally revealed the next major storyline in the heavyweight division. After suggesting Tom Aspinall might not be ready when the time comes, Pereira opened the door to a fascinating possibility: Josh Hokit. If Hokit defeats Derrick Lewis and capitalizes on the attention surrounding International Fight Week, could he suddenly find himself in the title conversation? Chael examines the paths available to Hokit, Alexander Volkov, and Sergei Pavlovich, why microphone skills still matter in MMA, and how quickly momentum can change in the heavyweight division. Plus, what happens if Tom Aspinall's return continues to be delayed? articles.title: The Most Effective Martial Art On An Airplane
articles.description: A viral airplane incident has reignited one of the oldest debates in combat sports: what is the most effective martial art in the real world? After a Jiu-Jitsu practitioner helped subdue a disruptive passenger on a flight, Chael Sonnen examines why certain martial arts translate so well to real-life situations and why many of the common arguments against combat sports simply don't hold up. From street fights and locker rooms to airplanes and the Octagon, Chael explains why training matters more than environment, why athleticism often goes unnoticed, and why Jiu-Jitsu remains one of the most practical fighting systems ever developed. articles.title: Chael Doesn't Buy Anthony Joshua's MMA Talk
articles.description: Anthony Joshua says he's interested in MMA, but Chael Sonnen isn't convinced. In Chael's view, this isn't about a serious career move—it's about attention. And if one of boxing's biggest stars is looking outside his sport, maybe the bigger question is why. Chael examines boxing's recent struggles against crossover competitors, from Conor McGregor vs Floyd Mayweather to Francis Ngannou's shocking performance against Tyson Fury. Along the way, Chael explains why he believes MMA owes Anthony Joshua nothing, why the UFC isn't likely to hand him a marquee opportunity, and why boxers rarely acknowledge the realities of crossing into another combat sport. Plus, Chael reveals the one boxer he believes could legitimately make the transition. articles.title: June 15 Changes Everything For Tom Aspinall
articles.description: Michael Bisping and Paul Felder's discussion about Tom Aspinall's eye injury sparked a much larger question: what exactly happens after June 14? Chael examines the timeline surrounding Aspinall's absence, the interim title fight between Alex Pereira and Ciryl Gane, and why the UFC's entire strategy depends on Tom remaining unavailable until that fight takes place. If Aspinall is healthy and ready, can the UFC still justify an interim championship? And if he isn't, how long can that continue before the division moves on? Chael also discusses Bisping's own experience with serious eye injuries, Eddie Hearn's controversial advice to Aspinall, and why June 15 could become the most important day in the heavyweight division. articles.title: If Ilia Topuria Loses…
articles.description: Justin Gaethje made a fascinating prediction: if he beats Ilia Topuria, Ilia will never fight again. Chael Sonnen couldn't disagree more. Using a lesson Daniel Cormier shared years ago, Chael explains the rare level of stardom where winning and losing no longer determines a fighter's future. According to Chael, Ilia Topuria has already reached that level. Whether he defeats Justin Gaethje or suffers his first UFC loss, the biggest fights in the sport will still be waiting. From a potential showdown with Islam Makhachev to the long-running rivalry with Paddy Pimblett, Chael breaks down why Topuria may actually have just as many opportunities in defeat as he does in victory. Plus, why Alex Pereira's career path provides a perfect example of how a loss can sometimes create even bigger possibilities. 0:00 Justin Gaethje's prediction1:15 Would Ilia retire after a loss?3:05 Daniel Cormier's lesson on greatness5:40 The highest level in MMA7:25 Why winning and losing doesn't matter9:15 Ilia vs Paddy Pimblett11:10 The Islam Makhachev possibilities13:00 Alex Pereira's career example15:20 Why losses can create opportunities17:05 Gaethje already proved it19:15 The UFC 300 comeback21:00 Why Ilia is already a superstar22:40 Chael's final answer Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Biggest Fight UFC Can Make? #ufc
articles.description: Rumors are flying about Arman Tsarukyan potentially fighting for the BMF title — but Chael Sonnen doesn’t believe that’s the direction the UFC is heading. Instead, Chael thinks the promotion may already have a much bigger plan in motion: Arman Tsarukyan vs. Ilia Topuria. In this breakdown, Chael explains why Tsarukyan is exactly the kind of fighter the UFC loves working with. He’ll fight anyone, at any weight class, under any circumstances — and according to Chael, that flexibility has quietly made Arman one of the most valuable contenders in the sport. He’s reportedly the backup fighter for the White House card, currently ranked at the top of the division, and appears to be next in line for an undisputed UFC title shot. But the most interesting part? Arman’s warning to Ilia Topuria. Chael reacts to Tsarukyan essentially telling the undefeated champion to go chase Islam Makhachev now — because if Ilia fights Arman first, that undefeated record may disappear. Chael believes Tsarukyan vs. Topuria has all the ingredients to become the UFC’s biggest event of the year: undefeated hype, championship stakes, stylistic danger, and real bad blood brewing underneath the surface. For more UFC analysis, MMA news, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily combat sports content. #UFC #IliaTopuria #ArmanTsarukyan #IslamMakhachev #ChaelSonnen #MMA #BMFTitle

articles.title: Nobody Wanted This Fight With Gable
articles.description: Gable Steveson finally has an opponent for his UFC debut, but Chael Sonnen believes the matchup raises more questions than answers. If the UFC truly evaluated Gable Steveson's skill level before selecting an opponent, what does this matchup tell us about where they think he is today? More importantly, why didn't a single heavyweight campaign for the opportunity? With Gable expected to receive one of the biggest spotlights of the year, Chael argues that standing across from him could have instantly changed another fighter's career. Chael breaks down the challenge of building a superstar prospect, the flaws in MMA rankings, and why some of the sport's biggest opportunities are often ignored by the very fighters who claim to want them. Plus, a look back at Khamzat Chimaev's rapid rise and what Gable Steveson's path to the top might actually look like. 0:00 Gable Steveson's UFC opponent revealed1:25 What the matchup tells us3:20 The UFC's three options5:15 Why top heavyweights weren't interested7:05 The problem with building prospects9:30 Khamzat Chimaev's rise11:45 Rankings vs reality13:50 Why prospects get stuck15:40 The opportunity nobody wanted18:00 What's next for Gable?20:05 A major flaw in MMA22:00 Chael's final takeaway Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: I just asked Alex Pereira About UFC champ, Tom Aspinal
articles.description: Chael reveals a surprising conversation with Alex Pereira that could have major implications for the UFC heavyweight division. While most fans assume the winner of Alex Pereira vs Ciryl Gane will eventually meet Tom Aspinall, Pereira isn't convinced Tom will be available when the time comes. According to Alex, his focus isn't just on beating Ciryl Gane—it's on maintaining the active schedule that has defined his UFC career. If Tom Aspinall isn't ready when Pereira wants to fight again, what happens next? Does the UFC ask the interim champion to wait? Does Pereira defend the interim belt? Could other heavyweight contenders suddenly find themselves in the title picture? Chael explores the ripple effects of Pereira's comments, why contract obligations may matter more than fans realize, and the difficult questions the UFC could soon be forced to answer. 0:00 Alex Pereira's surprising prediction1:30 Why Tom Aspinall may not be next3:05 Pereira's three-fights-a-year mentality4:45 The conversation with Ariel Helwani6:40 Why timing matters8:12 What if Tom isn't ready?10:05 Can an interim champion defend the belt?12:25 The Jon Jones precedent14:18 Potential heavyweight contenders16:05 Volkov, Pavlovich, and other options17:50 What happens if Alex demands another fight?19:30 The UFC's looming dilemma Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Rogan Raised a Huge Pereira Concern #ufc
articles.description: A lot of fans believe fighting at welterweight gives Conor McGregor a competitive advantage — but Chael Sonnen completely rejects that argument. In this breakdown, Chael explains why cutting to lightweight is actually proof of discipline, preparation, and focus, not a disadvantage. Chael responds to claims that Conor is somehow more dangerous at 170 pounds because he doesn’t have to suffer through a difficult weight cut. He compares the logic to fighters moving between every division in MMA, including featherweight, lightweight, and flyweight. According to Chael, once both athletes enter the cage on fight night, they weigh roughly the same anyway — so where exactly is the “advantage”? He also addresses comparisons involving Max Holloway moving up in weight and explains why fans misunderstand what weight classes are really testing. Chael’s core argument is simple: harder preparation creates sharper fighters. Suggesting that less work somehow equals a better competitive position completely misses the point of elite combat sports. This clip dives deep into weight cutting, MMA conditioning, fight preparation, and the psychology behind how fans evaluate size advantages in the UFC. For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #ConorMcGregor #UFC #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #Welterweight #CombatSports

articles.title: ARMAN’S ADVICE ON KHAMZAT’S FUTURE 😳🔥
articles.description: After Khamzat Chimaev’s loss to Sean Strickland, many fans are questioning whether “Borz” should stay at middleweight. Now Arman Tsarukyan has weighed in… 👀 Despite the difficult weight cut and title loss, Arman believes Khamzat can still compete at 185 pounds and remain a major force in the division. 🔥 But after what happened against Strickland, should Khamzat stay at middleweight or move up? 🤔 👇 WHAT’S NEXT FOR KHAMZAT?

articles.title: Overestimating Ilia... Or Underestimating Gaethje?
articles.description: Justin Gaethje is one of the most accomplished fighters in the sport, yet almost nobody is giving him a chance against Ilia Topuria. Why? Chael examines whether fans are overestimating Ilia Topuria, underestimating Justin Gaethje, or both. After dominant wins over Alexander Volkanovski, Charles Oliveira, and Max Holloway, Topuria has reached a level where many believe he's unbeatable. But has anyone really seen enough to make that claim? Chael breaks down Gaethje's resume, his skill set, and why only a handful of fighters have ever truly looked better than him inside the Octagon. Plus, a fascinating look at Daniel Cormier's definition of superstardom and why Ilia Topuria may have already reached the point where winning and losing no longer changes his status.
